message_header.subject.answer: Request for Temporary Remote Work Arrangement Due to Family Medical Situation

message.message.answer:
Dear Ms. Whitfield,

I am writing to formally request a temporary modification to my work arrangement, specifically to work remotely on a full‑time basis for a defined period due to an urgent family medical situation.

By way of background, I have been employed with Horizon Data Systems as a Senior Software Engineer in the Product Engineering department since August 2019. My primary responsibilities include backend API development for the Insight360 product line, participation in our on‑call rotation for production support, and mentorship of two junior engineers on my team. My normal work location is the Denver office at 1840 Blake Street, and my standard schedule is Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m.

Last week, on 2026-02-24, my mother, Elena Ramirez, who lives in Albuquerque, New Mexico, was hospitalized following complications from a recent knee replacement surgery. She developed a post‑operative infection and, as a result, is experiencing limited mobility and difficulty managing basic daily activities independently. Her physician has advised that she should not be left alone for extended periods, particularly over the next several weeks, as she undergoes IV antibiotic treatment and begins physical therapy.

My father passed away in 2021, and I am the closest adult child geographically and practically able to assist. My younger sister, who lives in Seattle, has two small children and limited flexibility to travel for an extended period. After discussing options with my family and my mother’s care team, it is clear that my presence in Albuquerque is the most practical way to ensure that she has consistent support with transportation to medical appointments, medication management, meal preparation, and general mobility around the home.

In light of these circumstances, I am requesting approval to work remotely on a full‑time basis from my mother’s residence in Albuquerque from 2026-03-09 through 2026-04-19 (a six‑week period). I selected these dates based on the estimated duration of her IV antibiotic course and the initial intensive phase of physical therapy, as outlined in the discharge summary provided by Presbyterian Hospital. If her recovery progresses more quickly than anticipated, I will gladly resume my normal in‑office schedule earlier, with appropriate notice.

I want to emphasize that I do not anticipate any negative impact on my productivity or on team deliverables during this period. Since the beginning of the COVID‑19 pandemic, I have successfully worked remotely for extended periods and have continued to meet or exceed expectations in terms of project deadlines, code quality, and responsiveness. Our team’s workflow is already heavily based on remote collaboration tools, including Slack, Zoom, Jira, and GitHub, and I am very comfortable working in that environment.

To ensure continuity of work, I have already taken the following steps:

1. I have confirmed with our IT department that I can securely access all necessary systems (VPN, code repositories, internal documentation, and monitoring dashboards) from my mother’s home internet connection. I have also tested a backup hotspot connection through my mobile carrier in case of any local outages.

2. I have spoken informally with my direct manager, Daniel Cho, about the situation. He indicated that he is generally supportive of a temporary remote arrangement, provided that it is formally documented and approved through HR. He also asked that I continue to participate fully in our daily stand‑ups at 9:15 a.m. Mountain Time and our weekly sprint planning and retrospective meetings, which I am fully able to do from Albuquerque.

3. I have reviewed our current sprint commitments and upcoming milestones. Most of my assigned tasks over the next two months involve backend feature development and bug fixes that can be performed entirely remotely. For the very few tasks that might require physical presence (for example, any ad‑hoc work with on‑premise test hardware), I have already coordinated with my teammate, Priya Patel, who has agreed to be the on‑site point of contact as needed.

4. I have set up a dedicated workspace at my mother’s house in a quiet spare bedroom, with a desk, ergonomic chair, external monitor, and reliable wired internet connection. I will be able to maintain regular business hours without interruption, as my mother’s daytime care needs are largely centered around transportation and meal preparation, which I can schedule around my breaks and lunch hour. For more time‑sensitive needs, my aunt, who lives nearby, has agreed to assist during my core work hours so that I remain fully available for meetings and focused work.

Regarding communication and availability, I commit to the following during the requested remote period:

- Maintain my standard 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. Mountain Time schedule, with a 30‑minute lunch break.
- Be reachable via Slack, email (michael.ramirez@horizondata.com), and phone at (303) 555‑9824 during all core hours.
- Keep my calendar up to date, clearly marking any medical appointments for my mother that might require brief schedule adjustments, and proactively informing my manager and team of any such changes.
- Continue participating fully in all team ceremonies, one‑on‑one meetings, and cross‑functional coordination calls.
- Provide weekly status updates to my manager summarizing progress on key tasks, any blockers, and plans for the following week.

I understand that the company has policies governing remote work arrangements and that approval is not automatic. I am more than willing to sign any required remote work agreements, complete any additional documentation, or provide supporting medical documentation regarding my mother’s condition if needed. I also understand that this request is for a temporary accommodation due to a specific family medical situation and does not imply a permanent change to my work location.

If it would be helpful, I would be glad to schedule a brief meeting with you and my manager to discuss this request in more detail, answer any questions, and address any concerns about logistics, performance expectations, or security considerations. My goal is to ensure that the company’s needs are fully met while also allowing me to be present for my mother during this critical phase of her recovery.

I greatly appreciate your time and consideration of this request, as well as the support that Horizon Data Systems has consistently shown for employees dealing with significant family responsibilities. Please let me know if there is a standard form, policy reference, or additional information you would like me to provide to facilitate a timely decision.

Thank you again for your understanding.

Sincerely,

Michael J. Ramirez
Senior Software Engineer
Horizon Data Systems
1840 Blake Street, Suite 500
Denver, CO 80202
Phone: (303) 555-9824
Email: michael.ramirez@horizondata.com

reply.reply.answer:
Approved with conditions.

Michael,

Thank you for providing such a clear and detailed explanation of your situation and for proactively outlining how you plan to manage your responsibilities while working remotely. After reviewing your request, discussing the proposed arrangement with your manager, Daniel Cho, and confirming that the necessary technical and security requirements can be met, we are able to approve a temporary remote work arrangement as outlined below.

1. APPROVAL PERIOD
Your request to work remotely from Albuquerque, New Mexico, is approved for the period from 2026-03-09 through 2026-04-19. This approval is temporary and is being granted specifically to accommodate your family medical situation. Any extension beyond 2026-04-19 will require a separate, written request submitted at least one week prior to the end of this period, along with an update on your mother’s condition and your anticipated caregiving responsibilities.

2. WORK SCHEDULE AND AVAILABILITY
You are expected to maintain your regular work schedule of Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m. Mountain Time, with a 30‑minute lunch break. As you proposed, you should remain reachable during core hours via Slack, email, and phone. Please ensure that your calendar accurately reflects any brief periods when you will be away from your desk for your mother’s medical appointments or other caregiving duties, and provide advance notice to your manager whenever possible.

3. PERFORMANCE EXPECTATIONS
All existing performance expectations remain in effect during the remote work period. You are expected to:

- Meet established deadlines and sprint commitments.
- Maintain the same level of responsiveness to your manager, teammates, and cross‑functional partners.
- Continue to produce work that meets our quality standards for code, documentation, and collaboration.
- Attend and actively participate in all required meetings, including daily stand‑ups, sprint planning, retrospectives, and one‑on‑one check‑ins.

Daniel will continue to monitor workload, deliverables, and communication to ensure that team objectives are met. If any performance or availability issues arise that appear to be directly impacted by the remote arrangement, we may revisit and adjust the terms of this approval.

4. EQUIPMENT AND SECURITY
You are responsible for ensuring that all company equipment (laptop, peripherals, and any other devices) used while working remotely is handled securely and in accordance with our Information Security and Remote Work policies. This includes:

- Using only the company‑issued laptop for work‑related activities.
- Accessing internal systems exclusively through the approved VPN connection.
- Ensuring that your work area at your mother’s residence is private enough to prevent unauthorized viewing or overhearing of confidential information.
- Locking your screen when stepping away and storing equipment securely when not in use.

If you encounter any technical issues while in Albuquerque, please contact the IT Help Desk as you normally would. You have indicated that you have already confirmed connectivity from your mother’s residence; if that situation changes, please inform IT and your manager promptly.

5. DOCUMENTATION
This message will serve as the written confirmation of your temporary remote work arrangement. No additional paperwork is required at this time. However, please note that HR may request basic supporting documentation regarding your mother’s medical situation for our confidential records, particularly if an extension beyond the initial six‑week period is requested. Any such documentation will be handled in accordance with our privacy and confidentiality standards.

6. CHECK‑INS AND REVIEW
We are asking that you and Daniel schedule a brief check‑in with HR approximately halfway through the approved period (on or around 2026-03-29) to review how the arrangement is working from both your perspective and the team’s perspective. The goal of this check‑in will be to confirm that expectations are being met and to address any adjustments that might be needed.

On behalf of Horizon Data Systems, I want to express our support during what I understand is a stressful time for you and your family. We appreciate your commitment to maintaining your work responsibilities while stepping up to care for your mother. If your circumstances change significantly—either improving more quickly than expected or becoming more complex—please keep your manager and HR informed so we can reassess as needed.

Please reply to this message via email to confirm that you understand and accept the terms outlined above. Once we receive your acknowledgment, we will update your employee record to reflect this temporary remote work arrangement.

Best regards,

Karen L. Whitfield
Human Resources Manager
Horizon Data Systems
Phone: (303) 555-4172
Email: karen.whitfield@horizondata.com
